Dr. Ian "Harley" Burbulis

B.S. in Biology, minor in biochemistry (Summa cum laude). Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, VA. 1993.

Ph.D. in Molecular and Cellular Biology. Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, VA. 1998.

Ph.D. thesis, "Macromolecular organization of flavonoid biosynthesis in Arabidopsis thaliana."

Joined the Molecular Sciences Institute in 1999.

Current research projects include developing methods to identify and quantify tens of proteins from complex mixtures simultaneously, creating new kinds of functional interaction maps in Saccharomyces cerevisiae, and understanding how macromolecular organization of kinases potentiate signaling pathways in living cells.
